re-measured for the one-time-line order: real CLI 'spex issues --all --json' (worktree cli, live github pull) → 52 issues, stores {github,local}, created strictly newest-first, stores interleaved (runs local5·gh1·local3·gh1·local13·gh1·local2·gh26 — the trailing gh block is genuinely the oldest issues); API /api/issues on a worktree backend :8799 gave the same order. Shapes unchanged (store tag, forge author/url/nodes[], local signers/replies)

real 'spex issues --all --json' in the live repo, forge reachable: 62 issues in ONE list (33 local + 29 github), interleaved by created newest-first (7 store transitions, sort verified programmatically), same shape both stores (forge issue: by=forge author, forge created, permalink url, Spec: markers pre-translated to nodes=[issues]; local thread: signers/replies/evidence). eval-remark threads split out read-time: 4 'concern: eval: …' threads exist in the raw trunk store, 0 in the list. --store/--node filter; default hides non-open (34 open).

throwaway repo (git init, no remote → gh listIssues fails): 'spex issues --all' exits 0, prints the FULL local list (2/2 threads), and stderr carries exactly ONE loud note 'spex issues: forge unreachable — listing local only (…)'. Local reading never hostaged on the network; degrade never silent.

YATU through the real CLI + real forge: open --store github created shuxueshuxue/spexcode#31 (gh view: OPEN, body carries 'Spec: issues', merged read shows re: issues via tracer); spex issues close github#31 → gh view state CLOSED. Local legs on a disposable store: open default + --store local committed, close resolved landed (idempotent repeat), unknown id / --store bogus fail loud with known-stores list, bare close prints usage exit 2.
Real forge smoke: promoted a disposable local issue to GitHub #32, verified local landed permalink reply, then closed github#32 and read state=CLOSED.

Re-measured at branch head (issues.ts unchanged since d1bee784): throwaway repo with origin pointing at a dead local path, two local threads in a disposable SPEXCODE_ISSUES_DIR — bare 'spex issues' prints the full local list on stdout and EXACTLY ONE loud stderr note ('forge unreachable — listing local only', 1 line total on stderr); the local read never hostages on the network and the degrade is never silent.
